reason: Both answers correctly identify the 100% exclusion (2016 acquisition, post-9/27/2010), confirm the $8M gain fits under the $10M cap, and correctly note no AMT preference and no NIIT on excluded gain — hitting all three key points. Both flag the same caveats: confirming QSBS eligibility conditions (original issuance, $50M gross assets test, active business) and state non-conformity (California). B is marginally more thorough (adds "no disqualifying redemptions") but this is a minor addition, not a meaningful differentiator, and A is slightly tighter. Substantively the two are equivalent in correctness and usefulness.
